certifications.title: SnowPro Core

Monitor and optimize performance
Enable data collaboration and protection

Transform, replicate, and share data across cloud platforms

Design scalable compute solutions for data engineer workloads
Evaluate performance metrics

Implement Snowpipe with pipes and notifications
Use streams + tasks for CDC pipelines
Create dependency-based task graphs
Optimize compute sizing and concurrency
Monitor with query history and warehouse usage
Manage replication and sharing patterns
Apply error handling and load history checks
certifications.candidateProfile: Recommended: 2+ years of hands-on experience as a Data Engineer in production.

certifications.title: SnowPro Advanced: Data Scientist
certifications.summary: Operationalize ML workflows with Snowpark, feature engineering, and governance.
certifications.detailSummary: Validates advanced data science principles, tools, and methodologies in Snowflake.
validates: Outline data science concepts
Implement Snowflake data science best practices

Package dependencies via stages and supported channels
Implement feature engineering in Snowflake
Train and evaluate ML models
Use UDFs and stored procedures appropriately
Apply governance with RBAC and masking

practiceQuestions.title: Micro-partition pruning
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which Snowflake capability reduces scanned data by skipping irrelevant micro-partitions?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Metadata-driven pruning limits scans to relevant micro-partitions.

practiceQuestions.title: Virtual warehouse sizing
practiceQuestions.prompt: What is the simplest first step when queries are consistently queued?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Right-size the warehouse or enable multi-cluster scaling to reduce queuing.

practiceQuestions.title: Snowpipe vs bulk load
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which Snowflake service continuously loads data from stages without a user-managed warehouse?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Snowpipe uses Snowflake-managed compute for continuous loading.

practiceQuestions.title: Pipe object essentials
practiceQuestions.prompt: What does a Snowpipe pipe object store to define loading behavior?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: A pipe stores the COPY statement and stage/table targets.

practiceQuestions.title: UDF versus Snowpark
practiceQuestions.prompt: When should you favor Snowpark over scalar SQL UDFs?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Use Snowpark for complex pipelines, batch processing, or ML workflows.

practiceQuestions.title: Model feature store
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which Snowflake capability helps centralize feature engineering and reuse across models?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Use governed tables/views and Snowpark pipelines for reusable features.

practiceQuestions.title: Network policies
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which control restricts access based on IP or network rules?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Network policies and rules limit where connections can originate.

practiceQuestions.title: Authentication policies
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which feature constrains authentication methods and client access?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Authentication policies enforce acceptable auth methods per user or account.

practiceQuestions.title: Query profile usage
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which UI tool helps diagnose where time is spent in a query plan?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: The Query Profile visualizes operators and bottlenecks.

practiceQuestions.title: Task orchestration
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which feature chains SQL work to run on schedules or after dependencies?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Tasks support schedules and dependency graphs.

practiceQuestions.title: Masking policies
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which policy type hides sensitive values at query time?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Masking policies redact data based on role context.

practiceQuestions.title: Feature engineering in Snowflake
practiceQuestions.prompt: Where should reusable features live for multiple models?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Store features in governed tables or views.

practiceQuestions.title: Time Travel basics
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which feature lets you query historical data within a retention window?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Time Travel supports point-in-time queries and recovery.

practiceQuestions.title: Fail-safe awareness
practiceQuestions.prompt: What happens after Time Travel ends for permanent tables?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Fail-safe provides a last-resort recovery period.

practiceQuestions.title: Zero-copy cloning
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which feature creates instant table copies without duplicating storage?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Zero-copy cloning shares underlying micro-partitions.

practiceQuestions.title: Result cache reuse
practiceQuestions.prompt: What must be true for a query to use the results cache?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Query text must match and underlying data must be unchanged.

practiceQuestions.title: Warehouse auto-suspend
practiceQuestions.prompt: Why enable auto-suspend on warehouses?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Auto-suspend reduces idle credit consumption.

practiceQuestions.title: Multi-cluster scaling
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which setting helps reduce queuing during concurrency spikes?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Multi-cluster scaling adds clusters for concurrent workloads.

practiceQuestions.title: Stages and file formats
practiceQuestions.prompt: Where do you define parsing rules for CSV or JSON loads?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: File formats encapsulate parsing and data type rules.

practiceQuestions.title: COPY INTO validation
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which command option helps validate load issues without ingesting data?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: VALIDATION_MODE checks files and errors without loading.

practiceQuestions.title: Semi-structured handling
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which data type stores JSON or nested data in Snowflake?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: VARIANT stores semi-structured data for flexible schemas.

practiceQuestions.title: Flatten usage
practiceQuestions.prompt: How do you expand arrays from a VARIANT column?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Use FLATTEN to explode arrays into rows.

practiceQuestions.title: Data sharing model
practiceQuestions.prompt: Who provides compute for data sharing queries?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Consumers use their own warehouses to query shared data.

practiceQuestions.title: Query history visibility
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which interface helps review query execution details and timings?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Query History and Query Profile explain runtime behavior.

practiceQuestions.title: Streams for CDC
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which object captures insert, update, and delete changes?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Streams record table change data for downstream processing.

practiceQuestions.title: Task dependencies
practiceQuestions.prompt: How do you chain SQL steps in a pipeline?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Tasks support dependency graphs for orchestration.

practiceQuestions.title: Dynamic tables purpose
practiceQuestions.prompt: What do dynamic tables simplify in ELT pipelines?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: They combine scheduling and change processing into one object.

practiceQuestions.title: Target lag control
practiceQuestions.prompt: What does TARGET_LAG define for a dynamic table?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: It sets how fresh the derived data should be.

practiceQuestions.title: Incremental refresh
practiceQuestions.prompt: When is incremental refresh best for dynamic tables?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Use it when changes impact only a small portion of data.

practiceQuestions.title: Snowpipe monitoring
practiceQuestions.prompt: Where do you monitor continuous load health?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Use load history and pipe status to detect ingestion issues.

practiceQuestions.title: Micro-partition clustering
practiceQuestions.prompt: Why add a clustering key to a large table?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Clustering improves pruning for common filters.

practiceQuestions.title: Clustering depth
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which metric helps evaluate clustering quality?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: SYSTEM$CLUSTERING_INFORMATION reports depth and partition stats.

practiceQuestions.title: Search optimization
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which feature accelerates selective point lookups?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Search optimization improves highly selective query access.

practiceQuestions.title: RBAC hierarchy
practiceQuestions.prompt: What is a best practice for role design?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Use least-privilege role hierarchies with grants to roles.

practiceQuestions.title: Resource monitors
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which feature enforces credit usage limits?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Resource monitors alert or suspend when thresholds are met.

practiceQuestions.title: Access history
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which view helps audit object access?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Access history supports governance and audit trails.

practiceQuestions.title: Replication & failover
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which features support disaster recovery across regions?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Replication and failover groups provide DR capability.

practiceQuestions.title: Network policies
practiceQuestions.prompt: How do you restrict login origins?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Network policies constrain access by IP ranges.

practiceQuestions.title: Snowpark DataFrames
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which API enables Pythonic transformations in Snowflake?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Snowpark DataFrames run transformations in-database.

practiceQuestions.title: Model training location
practiceQuestions.prompt: Where should model training occur to minimize data movement?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Train models inside Snowflake with governed data access.

practiceQuestions.title: UDF packaging
practiceQuestions.prompt: How are external dependencies delivered to Snowpark code?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Use stages and supported packages to bundle dependencies.

practiceQuestions.title: Feature governance
practiceQuestions.prompt: How do you keep features reusable and secure?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Use governed tables/views with RBAC and masking.

practiceQuestions.title: Model monitoring
practiceQuestions.prompt: Which practice helps monitor ML workload cost and performance?
practiceQuestions.takeaway: Track query history and warehouse usage for ML pipelines.

quizQuestions.question: Which Snowflake layer manages compute resources?
quizQuestions.explanation: Virtual warehouses provide compute for queries and loading.

quizQuestions.question: What is a primary benefit of multi-cluster warehouses?
quizQuestions.explanation: Multi-cluster warehouses scale out to handle concurrency.

quizQuestions.question: Which command is commonly used to load staged files into a table?
quizQuestions.explanation: COPY INTO loads data from a stage into a table.

quizQuestions.question: What does Time Travel allow you to do?
options: Access historical data within a retention window
quizQuestions.explanation: Time Travel enables querying and restoring historical data.

quizQuestions.question: Which object stores files before loading?
quizQuestions.explanation: Stages hold files for loading into Snowflake.

quizQuestions.question: What is the role of the cloud services layer?
options: Manages metadata, optimization, and access control
quizQuestions.explanation: Cloud services handle metadata, optimization, and security services.

quizQuestions.question: Which caching mechanism improves repeated query performance?
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache returns previously computed results when possible.

quizQuestions.question: What is the best way to manage semi-structured data?
quizQuestions.explanation: VARIANT and semi-structured functions support flexible schemas.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature protects data after Time Travel ends?
quizQuestions.explanation: Fail-safe provides a last-resort recovery period.

quizQuestions.question: Which object controls access using roles and grants?
quizQuestions.explanation: RBAC governs privileges and access to objects.

quizQuestions.question: What is a key benefit of micro-partitions?
quizQuestions.explanation: Micro-partitions enable automatic clustering and pruning.

quizQuestions.question: Which tool helps diagnose query performance?
quizQuestions.explanation: Query Profile visualizes query execution details.

quizQuestions.question: What does auto-suspend do for a warehouse?
quizQuestions.explanation: Auto-suspend pauses compute when idle.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature supports secure data sharing without copying?
quizQuestions.explanation: Secure data sharing lets consumers query shared data without copying.

quizQuestions.question: Which file format option improves load flexibility?
quizQuestions.explanation: FILE_FORMAT options control parsing and compression.

quizQuestions.question: What is the purpose of a named stage?
options: Store files for reuse in loads and unloads
quizQuestions.explanation: Named stages provide reusable locations for files.

quizQuestions.question: Which role is typically used for security administration?
quizQuestions.explanation: SECURITYADMIN manages roles and grants.

quizQuestions.question: What does clustering improve?
quizQuestions.explanation: Clustering optimizes partition pruning for query performance.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature controls credit usage?
quizQuestions.explanation: Resource monitors alert or suspend on credit thresholds.

quizQuestions.question: What is a key benefit of the result cache?
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache returns stored results for identical queries.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature captures table changes for downstream processing?
quizQuestions.explanation: Streams track DML changes for CDC pipelines.

quizQuestions.question: What does a task do?
options: Runs SQL on a schedule or dependency
quizQuestions.explanation: Tasks schedule SQL and dependency chains.

quizQuestions.question: Which service is recommended for continuous ingestion?
quizQuestions.explanation: Snowpipe provides continuous, serverless ingestion.

quizQuestions.question: What does a pipe store?
quizQuestions.explanation: Pipes define loading logic for Snowpipe.

quizQuestions.question: Which object helps manage incremental loads?
quizQuestions.explanation: Streams track new and changed rows.

quizQuestions.question: What is a best practice for task chains?
quizQuestions.explanation: Dependency graphs manage complex pipelines cleanly.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature supports near real-time transformations?
quizQuestions.explanation: Streams and tasks together enable incremental processing.

quizQuestions.question: When should you scale warehouse size up?
quizQuestions.explanation: Larger warehouses add compute for CPU-intensive workloads.

quizQuestions.question: Which view helps monitor load errors?
quizQuestions.explanation: LOAD_HISTORY provides status and errors for loads.

quizQuestions.question: What is a typical use for external tables?
options: Querying data in cloud storage without loading
quizQuestions.explanation: External tables let you query files in cloud storage.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement handles upserts efficiently?
quizQuestions.explanation: MERGE supports insert/update logic in one statement.

quizQuestions.question: How do you prevent duplicate loads with Snowpipe?
options: Use load metadata and file tracking
quizQuestions.explanation: Snowpipe tracks file metadata to avoid duplicates.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature provides continuous ingestion with cloud events?
quizQuestions.explanation: Auto-ingest integrates with cloud event notifications.

quizQuestions.question: Which view shows task execution history?
quizQuestions.explanation: TASK_HISTORY lists task runs and statuses.

quizQuestions.question: Which pattern supports replayable data pipelines?
quizQuestions.explanation: Keeping immutable raw data enables replay and recovery.

quizQuestions.question: What is a common reason to cluster a table?
quizQuestions.explanation: Clustering improves pruning for large datasets.

quizQuestions.question: Which object best supports incremental ETL?
quizQuestions.explanation: Streams are designed for change data capture.

quizQuestions.question: When should you use multi-cluster warehouses?
quizQuestions.explanation: Multi-cluster warehouses are for concurrent workloads.

quizQuestions.question: Which table type is best for transient staging data?
quizQuestions.explanation: Transient tables reduce storage costs for staging.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement helps validate file formats before load?
quizQuestions.explanation: VALIDATE can be used with COPY to check files.

quizQuestions.question: Which object stores reusable file location and credentials for loading?
quizQuestions.explanation: Stages define file locations and access for load/unload operations.

quizQuestions.question: Which COPY option helps continue loads when some rows are malformed?
quizQuestions.explanation: ON_ERROR controls behavior when row errors are encountered.

quizQuestions.question: What does Snowpipe auto-ingest rely on?
quizQuestions.explanation: Auto-ingest is triggered by cloud storage event notifications.

quizQuestions.question: Which view is best for auditing Snowpipe file loads?
quizQuestions.explanation: LOAD_HISTORY includes status and errors for file ingestion.

quizQuestions.question: What is the recommended pattern for idempotent loads?
options: Track files and ignore previously loaded files
quizQuestions.explanation: File tracking (Snowpipe or metadata) prevents duplicate loads.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature enables change data capture on tables?
quizQuestions.explanation: Streams track inserts, updates, and deletes.

quizQuestions.question: What is a best practice for continuous pipelines with tasks?
options: Schedule all tasks at the same minute
quizQuestions.explanation: Dependency graphs reduce conflicts and manage ordering.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement is used to create a pipe?
quizQuestions.explanation: Pipes are created with CREATE PIPE.

quizQuestions.question: Which file format setting controls field delimiters?
quizQuestions.explanation: FIELD_DELIMITER defines how fields are separated in CSV.

quizQuestions.question: Which view helps validate staged files before loading?
quizQuestions.explanation: VALIDATION_MODE enables previewing load errors.

quizQuestions.question: What is the primary purpose of a landing/raw zone?
quizQuestions.explanation: Immutable raw data supports replay and auditing.

quizQuestions.question: Which object can load data directly from cloud storage without staging?
quizQuestions.explanation: External tables query files in cloud storage without loading.

quizQuestions.question: What does COPY INTO return when used with VALIDATION_MODE?
quizQuestions.explanation: VALIDATION_MODE checks files and returns errors.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature helps prevent duplicate file processing in Snowpipe?
quizQuestions.explanation: Snowpipe tracks file metadata to ensure idempotency.

quizQuestions.question: Which task type uses warehouse compute?
quizQuestions.explanation: User-managed tasks run on a specified warehouse.

quizQuestions.question: What is a best practice for handling schema evolution in files?
options: Use file formats and staged raw columns
quizQuestions.explanation: Staging raw data and parsing helps handle evolving schemas.

quizQuestions.question: Which command lists files in a stage?
quizQuestions.explanation: LIST returns file names stored in a stage.

quizQuestions.question: Which Snowflake object stores COPY logic for continuous ingestion?
quizQuestions.explanation: Pipes encapsulate COPY statements for Snowpipe.

quizQuestions.question: What should you monitor to detect stalled ingestion?
quizQuestions.explanation: Load history and pipe usage indicate ingestion health.

quizQuestions.question: Which pattern supports near real-time transformations from streams?
quizQuestions.explanation: Streams with tasks enable near real-time processing.

quizQuestions.question: Which tool is best for identifying where time is spent in a query?
quizQuestions.explanation: Query Profile breaks down query execution steps.

quizQuestions.question: What does the result cache require to be reused?
options: Identical query text and unchanged data
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache is used when the same query runs on unchanged data.

quizQuestions.question: When should you use a multi-cluster warehouse?
quizQuestions.explanation: Multi-cluster warehouses scale out to handle concurrency.

quizQuestions.question: What is the main benefit of clustering keys?
quizQuestions.explanation: Clustering improves pruning for large, skewed tables.

quizQuestions.question: Which setting improves throughput for CPU-bound queries?
quizQuestions.explanation: Larger warehouses provide more compute.

quizQuestions.question: What does warehouse auto-suspend reduce?
quizQuestions.explanation: Auto-suspend stops warehouses when idle to save credits.

quizQuestions.question: Which view helps identify long-running queries?
quizQuestions.explanation: QUERY_HISTORY includes duration and status.

quizQuestions.question: What is a typical reason to add a clustering key?
options: Large tables with frequent range predicates
quizQuestions.explanation: Clustering helps when pruning is poor on large tables.

quizQuestions.question: Which cache stores metadata about micro-partitions?
quizQuestions.explanation: Metadata cache speeds partition pruning.

quizQuestions.question: What does scaling out a multi-cluster warehouse address?
quizQuestions.explanation: Scaling out adds clusters to handle concurrency.

quizQuestions.question: Which metric helps assess cache effectiveness?
quizQuestions.explanation: Query history exposes whether results came from cache.

quizQuestions.question: Which action reduces scan size for selective filters?
quizQuestions.explanation: Pruning reduces scanned partitions for selective filters.

quizQuestions.question: What is the benefit of auto-resume?
quizQuestions.explanation: Auto-resume starts warehouses when queries arrive.

quizQuestions.question: Which query pattern is most likely to bypass result cache?
quizQuestions.explanation: Non-deterministic functions prevent cache reuse.

quizQuestions.question: What helps reduce contention on shared warehouses?
quizQuestions.explanation: Isolating workloads avoids resource contention.

quizQuestions.question: Which account usage view supports warehouse credit analysis?
quizQuestions.explanation: WAREHOUSE_METERING_HISTORY shows credit consumption.

quizQuestions.question: Which action helps when storage is scanned excessively?
quizQuestions.explanation: Better pruning and clustering reduce scanned data.

quizQuestions.question: What is a typical reason for query queuing?
quizQuestions.explanation: Queuing occurs when warehouses are fully utilized.

quizQuestions.question: Which technique helps monitor pipeline performance end-to-end?
options: Combine TASK_HISTORY, QUERY_HISTORY, and load metrics
quizQuestions.explanation: History views provide visibility into pipeline execution.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature helps accelerate point lookups on large tables?
quizQuestions.explanation: Search optimization improves selective point lookups.

quizQuestions.question: What is the typical compressed size of a micro-partition?
quizQuestions.explanation: Micro-partitions are stored in ~16 MB compressed blocks.

quizQuestions.question: Which metadata enables automatic partition pruning?
quizQuestions.explanation: Cloud services stores min/max stats for pruning.

quizQuestions.question: Partition elimination in Snowflake relies primarily on:
quizQuestions.explanation: Snowflake uses metadata for pruning without indexes.

quizQuestions.question: What does clustering depth represent?
options: Average micro-partitions scanned for a value
quizQuestions.explanation: Clustering depth estimates the scan breadth per value.

quizQuestions.question: Which function reports clustering statistics?
quizQuestions.explanation: SYSTEM$CLUSTERING_INFORMATION reports depth and partition stats.

quizQuestions.question: When are cluster keys most justified?
quizQuestions.explanation: Clustering benefits large tables with heavy read workloads.

quizQuestions.question: Which workload pattern is a poor fit for clustering keys?
quizQuestions.explanation: Frequent updates disrupt clustering and increase recluster cost.

quizQuestions.question: Which cluster key choice is typically a mistake?
quizQuestions.explanation: High-cardinality keys increase reclustering with little benefit.

quizQuestions.question: How can you reduce clustering key cardinality for timestamps?
options: Cluster by DATE_TRUNC on the timestamp
quizQuestions.explanation: Truncating timestamps reduces cardinality while preserving filters.

quizQuestions.question: What effect can Snowpipe have on natural clustering?
options: It can disrupt natural clustering order
quizQuestions.explanation: Streaming loads can disrupt naturally clustered ordering.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about reclustering is true?
options: It runs in the background and is billed separately
It locks the table for reads
quizQuestions.explanation: Automatic reclustering runs in the background with separate billing.

quizQuestions.question: Which command adds accounts to a share?
quizQuestions.explanation: ALTER SHARE adds one or more accounts to a share.

quizQuestions.question: Is there a hard limit on the number of accounts in a share?
quizQuestions.explanation: There is no hard limit on accounts in a share.

quizQuestions.question: Which cloud platforms support cross-region data sharing?
quizQuestions.explanation: Cross-region sharing is supported across AWS, Azure, and GCP.

quizQuestions.question: What must be granted to a share before consumers can access data?
options: Privileges on schemas, tables, and secure views
quizQuestions.explanation: Shares must include privileges on objects to be accessible.

quizQuestions.question: When objects are added to a share, they become available:
quizQuestions.explanation: Objects are instantly available to consumer accounts.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement is true about data sharing?
options: Shared data is copied into consumer accounts
Shared data stays in provider storage
quizQuestions.explanation: Shared data remains in the provider account with no copying.

quizQuestions.question: Which object do consumers create from a share to access data?
quizQuestions.explanation: Consumers create a database from the share.

quizQuestions.question: What is required to share data with another account?
options: Create share and grant object privileges
quizQuestions.explanation: You must create a share and grant privileges to objects.

quizQuestions.question: Which is a valid approach to share data across cloud regions?
quizQuestions.explanation: Snowflake supports cross-region data sharing across clouds.

quizQuestions.question: Which command can add an additional account to an existing share?
quizQuestions.explanation: ALTER SHARE adds accounts to a share.

quizQuestions.question: Data sharing in Snowflake requires which compute?
quizQuestions.explanation: Consumers use their own warehouses to query shared data.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement best describes reader accounts?
options: Accounts hosted by Snowflake for consumers
Accounts that only read local files
Accounts that cannot query shared data
quizQuestions.explanation: Reader accounts let non-Snowflake customers access shared data.

quizQuestions.question: Which objects can be shared?
quizQuestions.explanation: Shares include schemas, tables, and secure views.

quizQuestions.question: Adding objects to a share requires:
quizQuestions.explanation: Use GRANT <privilege> ... TO SHARE.

quizQuestions.question: Which is NOT required for data sharing?
quizQuestions.explanation: Data sharing does not require copying data files.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about data sharing is true?
options: Shared data is duplicated into consumer storage
Shared data is accessed via consumer-created databases
Shared data requires public internet access
quizQuestions.explanation: Consumers create databases from the share to access data.

quizQuestions.question: What is a recommended action for a long-running query?
options: Scale up the warehouse and resubmit the query
quizQuestions.explanation: Scaling up and resubmitting can improve long-running query performance.

quizQuestions.question: Result cache can be used when:
options: The query is identical and data hasn't changed within 24 hours
The query is different but uses same tables
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache works for identical queries within 24 hours with unchanged data.

quizQuestions.question: When should re-clustering be considered?
options: When table reaches ~1TB and queries slow
quizQuestions.explanation: Best practice: consider re-clustering when large tables become slow.

quizQuestions.question: Multi-cluster warehouses are designed for:
quizQuestions.explanation: They address queuing and concurrency issues.

quizQuestions.question: Dedicated warehouses can help by:
quizQuestions.explanation: Dedicated warehouses isolate workloads and reduce contention.

quizQuestions.question: How long is query history available in the web UI?
quizQuestions.explanation: Query history is available for 14 days.

quizQuestions.question: Scaling up a warehouse primarily affects:
quizQuestions.explanation: Scaling up improves performance for individual queries.

quizQuestions.question: Result cache is invalidated when:
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache requires unchanged underlying data.

quizQuestions.question: Which approach helps reduce queuing?
quizQuestions.explanation: Multi-cluster warehouses reduce queuing for concurrent workloads.

quizQuestions.question: What is a sign to consider scaling up?
quizQuestions.explanation: Scaling up can help long-running queries complete faster.

quizQuestions.question: Why might you use separate warehouses for ETL and BI?
quizQuestions.explanation: Dedicated warehouses separate workloads and improve performance.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about clustering is true?
quizQuestions.explanation: Clustering is typically considered for large tables.

quizQuestions.question: Which caching type depends on identical query text?
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache requires identical query text.

quizQuestions.question: What should you do after scaling up to test improvement?
quizQuestions.explanation: Resubmit the query to evaluate performance changes.

quizQuestions.question: Which metric is NOT needed for result cache eligibility?
quizQuestions.explanation: Warehouse size changes are not required for result cache.

quizQuestions.question: Multi-cluster warehouses help most with:
quizQuestions.explanation: They are designed for concurrent workloads.

quizQuestions.question: Which scenario aligns with scaling out?
options: Adding a separate warehouse for BI
quizQuestions.explanation: Scaling out adds separate warehouses for workloads.

quizQuestions.question: Query history availability in the web UI is:
quizQuestions.explanation: Query history is available for 14 days.

quizQuestions.question: Which is a best practice for long running queries?
options: Use a larger warehouse and resubmit
quizQuestions.explanation: Scaling up and resubmitting can help performance.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about dedicated warehouses is true?
quizQuestions.explanation: Dedicated warehouses isolate workloads.

quizQuestions.question: Which cache is shared across warehouses and users?
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache is global and can be reused across warehouses.

quizQuestions.question: What invalidates the result cache for a query?
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache requires unchanged underlying data.

quizQuestions.question: Which action clears the warehouse data cache?
quizQuestions.explanation: Suspending a warehouse clears its local data cache.

quizQuestions.question: Where does the metadata cache live?
quizQuestions.explanation: Metadata cache is stored in the cloud services layer.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about the warehouse data cache is true?
options: It persists while the warehouse runs and ages out by LRU
It is shared across all warehouses
It is stored in cloud storage
quizQuestions.explanation: Warehouse cache is local and uses LRU-style eviction.

quizQuestions.question: Which session setting disables the results cache for testing?
quizQuestions.explanation: USE_CACHED_RESULT can be set to FALSE during benchmarks.

quizQuestions.question: A query served from the result cache typically needs:
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache can return results without running compute.

quizQuestions.question: What does AUTO_SUSPEND = 0 imply?
quizQuestions.explanation: AUTO_SUSPEND=0 keeps the warehouse running until stopped.

quizQuestions.question: Which AUTO_SUSPEND value is commonly recommended for balance?
quizQuestions.explanation: A short auto-suspend (around 60s) balances cost and cache benefit.

quizQuestions.question: What happens to the warehouse cache after resizing the warehouse?
options: It is stored in metadata cache
quizQuestions.explanation: Resizing a warehouse clears its local data cache.

quizQuestions.question: Which cache helps COUNT(*) return quickly on large tables?
quizQuestions.explanation: Metadata cache uses partition stats to answer counts quickly.

quizQuestions.question: Which condition is NOT required for result cache reuse?
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache can be reused across warehouse sizes.

quizQuestions.question: Which cache is most helpful for a second run immediately after a cold run?
quizQuestions.explanation: A warm run benefits from data cached in the warehouse.

quizQuestions.question: Which cache is stored in the cloud services layer?
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache resides in the cloud services layer.

quizQuestions.question: Which function can return the previous query result set?
quizQuestions.explanation: RESULT_SCAN can read a previous result set for reuse.

quizQuestions.question: Which are the three table types in Snowflake?
quizQuestions.explanation: Snowflake tables are permanent, transient, or temporary.

quizQuestions.question: Temporary tables persist for:
quizQuestions.explanation: Temporary tables exist only for the session in which they are created.

quizQuestions.question: Transient and temporary tables have:
quizQuestions.explanation: Transient and temporary tables do not have Fail-safe.

quizQuestions.question: Default Time Travel retention for transient/temporary tables is:
quizQuestions.explanation: Default Time Travel retention for transient/temporary tables is 1 day.

quizQuestions.question: What is a key difference between standard and materialized views?
quizQuestions.explanation: Materialized views store results, improving access speed.

quizQuestions.question: Snowflake handles backups as part of:
quizQuestions.explanation: Snowflake manages backups automatically.

quizQuestions.question: Storage costs are calculated based on:
options: Daily average storage for the billing month
quizQuestions.explanation: Storage cost is based on daily average storage.

quizQuestions.question: Micro-partitions typically contain:
quizQuestions.explanation: Micro-partitions contain about 50-500 MB of uncompressed data.

quizQuestions.question: Which metadata does Snowflake store for micro-partitions?
options: Range of values and number of distinct values
quizQuestions.explanation: Micro-partitions store range and distinct value metadata for pruning.

quizQuestions.question: What is the most efficient way to copy production objects for testing?
quizQuestions.explanation: Cloning uses zero data movement and minimal storage.

quizQuestions.question: Updating cloned objects results in:
quizQuestions.explanation: Changes to clones consume additional storage for new row versions.

quizQuestions.question: Fail-safe is:
options: A 7-day recovery period managed by Snowflake
quizQuestions.explanation: Fail-safe is a 7-day recovery period managed by Snowflake.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about temporary tables is true?
quizQuestions.explanation: Temporary tables are session-scoped and not visible to others.

quizQuestions.question: Which view type stores data physically for faster access?
quizQuestions.explanation: Materialized views store results physically.

quizQuestions.question: Snowflake automatically manages micro-partitions to:
options: Enable pruning without manual DBA work
quizQuestions.explanation: Automatic management enables efficient pruning without manual indexing.

quizQuestions.question: Which is true about micro-partition metadata?
options: Used for optimization and query processing
quizQuestions.explanation: Metadata supports optimization and efficient query processing.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about transient tables is correct?
options: No Fail-safe and lower storage costs
quizQuestions.explanation: Transient tables avoid Fail-safe and lower storage costs.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature enables pruning for semi-structured data?
quizQuestions.explanation: Micro-partition metadata enables precise pruning.

quizQuestions.question: Fail-safe is intended for:
options: Snowflake to recover from extreme operational failures
quizQuestions.explanation: Fail-safe is for Snowflake recovery after extreme failures.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about cloning is true?
options: Zero data movement and fast creation
quizQuestions.explanation: Cloning is efficient and uses zero data movement.

quizQuestions.question: Are there additional costs for Streams or Tasks?
quizQuestions.explanation: The guide notes no additional costs for Streams or Tasks.

quizQuestions.question: A task can execute:
options: A single SQL statement or stored procedure call
Multiple SQL statements without a procedure
quizQuestions.explanation: Tasks run a single SQL statement or stored procedure call.

quizQuestions.question: Tasks must initially be triggered by:
quizQuestions.explanation: Tasks are initially triggered on a schedule.

quizQuestions.question: Which is required when creating a task?
quizQuestions.explanation: A task needs a name, a SQL statement, and a warehouse.

quizQuestions.question: Child tasks can be called from:
quizQuestions.explanation: Child tasks can only be called from a parent task.

quizQuestions.question: All tasks in a simple tree must:
options: Share the same owner role and schema
quizQuestions.explanation: Tasks in a tree must share owner and schema.

quizQuestions.question: What does ALTER TABLE ... CHANGE_TRACKING = TRUE do?
options: Adds hidden columns for change tracking
quizQuestions.explanation: It adds hidden columns and starts tracking changes.

quizQuestions.question: To capture CDC changes, you must create:
options: A stream on the source table
quizQuestions.explanation: Streams capture changes from source tables.

quizQuestions.question: Which system function indicates if a stream has data?
quizQuestions.explanation: SYSTEM$STREAM_HAS_DATA indicates CDC records.

quizQuestions.question: The stream offset represents:
options: A bookmark to the stream position
quizQuestions.explanation: Offsets are bookmarks that advance when the stream is consumed.

quizQuestions.question: When is the stream position updated?
options: At the end of the transaction
quizQuestions.explanation: Positions update at the end of the transaction.

quizQuestions.question: What does METADATA$ISUPDATE indicate?
options: Whether a record is part of an UPDATE
quizQuestions.explanation: METADATA$ISUPDATE indicates update actions.

quizQuestions.question: A CDC pipeline using streams typically requires:
options: Stream, target table, and task or procedure
quizQuestions.explanation: CDC uses streams with tasks/procedures to merge into target tables.

quizQuestions.question: Tasks are used to:
options: Run SQL statements in a defined order
quizQuestions.explanation: Tasks chain SQL statements in defined workflows.

quizQuestions.question: Child tasks are triggered:
quizQuestions.explanation: Child tasks run after their parent task completes.

quizQuestions.question: Change tracking adds:
quizQuestions.explanation: Change tracking adds hidden columns to track DML.

quizQuestions.question: Tasks must be stored in:
options: The same database and schema for a task tree
quizQuestions.explanation: Task trees must be in the same database and schema.

quizQuestions.question: Which is required to run a task?
quizQuestions.explanation: Tasks require a warehouse to execute.

quizQuestions.question: Streams are used to:
quizQuestions.explanation: Streams capture DML changes for CDC workflows.

quizQuestions.question: Tasks can call:
quizQuestions.explanation: Tasks can execute a stored procedure call.

quizQuestions.question: Semi-structured data should be loaded into which data type?
quizQuestions.explanation: The VARIANT data type stores semi-structured data.

quizQuestions.question: VARIANT can store which data structures?
quizQuestions.explanation: VARIANT can store OBJECT and ARRAY values.

quizQuestions.question: What is the maximum size for VARIANT values?
quizQuestions.explanation: VARIANT values can be up to 16 MB compressed.

quizQuestions.question: Best practice for BI tools accessing VARIANT data is to:
quizQuestions.explanation: Flattened views make semi-structured data accessible to BI tools.

quizQuestions.question: Which function creates JSON output from relational data?
quizQuestions.explanation: OBJECT_CONSTRUCT builds JSON objects.

quizQuestions.question: VARIANT is best described as:
quizQuestions.explanation: VARIANT is a tagged universal type.

quizQuestions.question: Semi-structured data pruning is enabled by:
quizQuestions.explanation: Micro-partition metadata enables pruning for semi-structured data.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about VARIANT is true?
options: It can store values of any type
quizQuestions.explanation: VARIANT can store values of any type, including arrays and objects.

quizQuestions.question: A common approach to expose VARIANT fields for analytics is:
options: Flatten into columns via a view
Move data to an external stage
quizQuestions.explanation: Views with flattening provide structured access to VARIANT data.

quizQuestions.question: Which is a recommended storage type for JSON?
quizQuestions.explanation: JSON should be stored in a VARIANT column.

quizQuestions.question: What does OBJECT_CONSTRUCT produce?
quizQuestions.explanation: OBJECT_CONSTRUCT creates JSON objects.

quizQuestions.question: Which approach helps BI tools consume semi-structured data?
quizQuestions.explanation: Flattened views make semi-structured data accessible.

quizQuestions.question: VARIANT values are limited to:
quizQuestions.explanation: VARIANT supports up to 16 MB compressed.

quizQuestions.question: Which is a common transformation for semi-structured data?
quizQuestions.explanation: Flattening makes semi-structured data usable in analytics.

quizQuestions.question: Which data type is best suited to store arrays in Snowflake?
quizQuestions.explanation: VARIANT supports arrays and objects.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about semi-structured data is correct?
options: Load into VARIANT and query with Snowflake functions
quizQuestions.explanation: Snowflake recommends loading semi-structured data into VARIANT.

quizQuestions.question: Which is a valid reason to create a view for VARIANT data?
options: To present flattened data to BI tools
quizQuestions.explanation: Views can flatten VARIANT for BI consumption.

quizQuestions.question: VARIANT is best described as:
options: A universal type for semi-structured data
quizQuestions.explanation: VARIANT is a universal type for semi-structured data.

quizQuestions.question: Which function helps build JSON from relational data?
quizQuestions.explanation: OBJECT_CONSTRUCT produces JSON objects.

quizQuestions.question: Which is true about VARIANT size?
quizQuestions.explanation: VARIANT values can be up to 16 MB compressed.

quizQuestions.question: Which account-level object stores IAM configuration for external stages?
quizQuestions.explanation: Storage integrations store IAM identities and allowed locations for external access.

quizQuestions.question: Search Optimization Service is primarily used to:
quizQuestions.explanation: Search optimization improves performance for selective lookups.

quizQuestions.question: When is the result cache reused?
options: Same query text with unchanged data
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache is reused when the query text matches and underlying data is unchanged.

quizQuestions.question: What does METADATA$FILENAME provide in staged data?
quizQuestions.explanation: METADATA$FILENAME returns the path and name of the staged file.

quizQuestions.question: Which policy enforces row-level security in Snowflake?
quizQuestions.explanation: Row access policies filter rows based on context.

quizQuestions.question: Which ACCOUNT_USAGE view shows warehouse credit consumption?
quizQuestions.explanation: WAREHOUSE_METERING_HISTORY reports warehouse credits.

quizQuestions.question: Which file format is columnar and commonly used for analytics?
quizQuestions.explanation: Parquet is a columnar, analytics-friendly format.

quizQuestions.question: Which COPY INTO option continues loading valid rows while skipping errors?
quizQuestions.explanation: ON_ERROR = CONTINUE loads valid rows and skips errors.

quizQuestions.question: Which system role is primarily responsible for managing users and roles?
quizQuestions.explanation: USERADMIN manages users and roles in Snowflake.

quizQuestions.question: What is the maximum Time Travel retention in Enterprise Edition?
quizQuestions.explanation: Enterprise Edition supports up to 90 days of Time Travel.

quizQuestions.question: How is Fail-safe recovery performed?
quizQuestions.explanation: Fail-safe recovery requires a Snowflake support request.

quizQuestions.question: Which view type is required for secure data sharing?
quizQuestions.explanation: Secure views are required for sharing data safely.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature enables continuous file ingestion with cloud notifications?
quizQuestions.explanation: Snowpipe auto-ingest uses cloud events for continuous ingestion.

quizQuestions.question: Which function reports clustering information for a table?
quizQuestions.explanation: SYSTEM$CLUSTERING_INFORMATION returns clustering metrics.

quizQuestions.question: Which table type does not support Fail-safe?
quizQuestions.explanation: Transient tables do not include Fail-safe.

quizQuestions.question: Multi-cluster warehouses primarily help with:
quizQuestions.explanation: Multi-cluster warehouses scale out for concurrency.

quizQuestions.question: What does a stream provide for downstream processing?
quizQuestions.explanation: Streams track DML changes for CDC pipelines.

quizQuestions.question: Which syntax reads the first column from a staged file?
quizQuestions.explanation: $1 references the first column from staged files.

quizQuestions.question: Secure data sharing allows consumers to:
quizQuestions.explanation: Sharing enables read-only access without copying data.

quizQuestions.question: Which command can be used to review retention-related parameters?
quizQuestions.explanation: SHOW PARAMETERS reveals retention settings at account/db/schema/table levels.

quizQuestions.question: Which notation refers to a user’s internal stage?
quizQuestions.explanation: @~ references the user stage.

quizQuestions.question: How do you specify file format options for a user stage?
quizQuestions.explanation: User stages require file format options in COPY INTO.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about external tables is true?
options: They are read-only over external data
quizQuestions.explanation: External tables are read-only and reference external data.

quizQuestions.question: How do you enable a directory table for a stage?
quizQuestions.explanation: DIRECTORY = (ENABLE = TRUE) creates a directory table for a stage.

quizQuestions.question: Which function generates a temporary browser-accessible URL to a stage file?
quizQuestions.explanation: GET_PRESIGNED_URL returns a temporary URL for stage files.

quizQuestions.question: Which function produces a secure URL for stage file sharing inside Snowflake?
quizQuestions.explanation: BUILD_SCOPED_FILE_URL creates a scoped URL for secure access.

quizQuestions.question: Which COPY INTO validation mode returns all errors without loading data?
quizQuestions.explanation: RETURN_ERRORS validates files and returns errors only.

quizQuestions.question: Which task condition runs only when a stream has new data?
quizQuestions.explanation: SYSTEM$STREAM_HAS_DATA gates task execution on stream changes.

quizQuestions.question: Which command creates a pipe with automatic ingestion?
quizQuestions.explanation: AUTO_INGEST is a pipe property for Snowpipe.

quizQuestions.question: Which command restores a dropped table within Time Travel?
quizQuestions.explanation: UNDROP restores objects within the retention window.

quizQuestions.question: What is true about zero-copy cloning?
options: It is metadata-only until changes occur
quizQuestions.explanation: Zero-copy cloning initially shares metadata and storage.

quizQuestions.question: Resource monitors are used to:
options: Notify or suspend when credit limits are reached
quizQuestions.explanation: Resource monitors manage credit usage with alerts and suspensions.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature helps label queries for tracking in history views?
quizQuestions.explanation: QUERY_TAG labels queries for filtering in history.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about materialized views is true?
options: They store results and have refresh history
quizQuestions.explanation: Materialized views store results and expose refresh metadata.

quizQuestions.question: External functions require which Snowflake object?
quizQuestions.explanation: External functions use API integrations for secure calls.

quizQuestions.question: Which data type is commonly used to store full JSON objects?
quizQuestions.explanation: VARIANT stores semi-structured JSON documents.

quizQuestions.question: Which construct expands arrays into rows for querying?
quizQuestions.explanation: LATERAL FLATTEN expands arrays or objects into rows.

quizQuestions.question: Secure views execute using which role context?
quizQuestions.explanation: Secure views execute with the owner’s role context.

quizQuestions.question: What does a network policy control?
quizQuestions.explanation: Network policies restrict access by IP or network rules.

quizQuestions.question: Warehouse scaling policy (ECONOMY vs STANDARD) affects:
options: How aggressively clusters are added or removed
quizQuestions.explanation: Scaling policy controls cluster ramp-up and scale-down behavior.

quizQuestions.question: Which change most likely invalidates the result cache for a query?
options: Using a larger result set cache
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache is invalidated when underlying data changes.

quizQuestions.question: Which query is least likely to reuse the result cache?
quizQuestions.explanation: Non-deterministic functions (like CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) bypass result cache reuse.

quizQuestions.question: Secure views can reduce performance primarily because they:
quizQuestions.explanation: Secure views bypass some optimizations to protect metadata and logic.

quizQuestions.question: Which is true about Fail-safe?
options: Only Snowflake support can restore data
It is available for transient tables
quizQuestions.explanation: Fail-safe recovery is handled by Snowflake support and is not user-configurable.

quizQuestions.question: Time Travel retention is controlled by:
quizQuestions.explanation: DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS defines Time Travel retention.

quizQuestions.question: A common mistake with secure views is assuming they:
options: Provide the same performance as standard views
quizQuestions.explanation: Secure views may perform slower than standard views.

quizQuestions.question: Which action does NOT invalidate the result cache?
options: Re-running the same query with identical text
quizQuestions.explanation: If data is unchanged and query text is identical, result cache can be reused.

quizQuestions.question: Time Travel does NOT apply to:
quizQuestions.explanation: External tables do not use Time Travel.

quizQuestions.question: Which cache stores table micro-partition metadata?
quizQuestions.explanation: Metadata cache stores micro-partition metadata for pruning.

quizQuestions.question: Fail-safe is available for:
quizQuestions.explanation: Fail-safe applies to permanent objects.

quizQuestions.question: Which is a misconception about Time Travel?
options: It is free and has no storage cost
It can be configured per table
quizQuestions.explanation: Time Travel retains historical data and increases storage.

quizQuestions.question: A query with a non-deterministic UDF will likely:
quizQuestions.explanation: Non-deterministic logic prevents safe result cache reuse.

quizQuestions.question: Which statement about secure views is correct?
options: They execute as the owner role
quizQuestions.explanation: Secure views execute with the owner role context.

quizQuestions.question: Which scenario requires contacting Snowflake support?
quizQuestions.explanation: Fail-safe recovery requires Snowflake support.

quizQuestions.question: Which action can accidentally disable Time Travel?
quizQuestions.explanation: Retention of 0 effectively disables Time Travel.

quizQuestions.question: Which is true about query result cache?
options: It is tied to a single warehouse
It works only for materialized views
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache is not tied to a warehouse and can be reused across warehouses.

quizQuestions.question: Which is a common pitfall with secure views and sharing?
options: Expecting secure views to behave like standard views for optimization
Thinking secure views cannot be shared
quizQuestions.explanation: Secure views may have different performance characteristics.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature continues beyond Time Travel by 7 days?
quizQuestions.explanation: Fail-safe provides 7 additional days of recovery after Time Travel.

quizQuestions.question: A query text change (like whitespace or comments) will:
quizQuestions.explanation: Result cache reuse requires identical query text.

quizQuestions.question: Which is true about Fail-safe storage costs?
options: It adds storage cost for the extra retention period
It is only for transient tables
quizQuestions.explanation: Fail-safe retains historical data, increasing storage usage.

quizQuestions.question: You need continuous ingestion from cloud storage with minimal ops. Best choice?
quizQuestions.explanation: Snowpipe auto-ingest provides continuous ingestion with minimal ops.

quizQuestions.question: You need to share data to another Snowflake account without copying. Best design?
options: Secure data share with secure views
Copy tables into a new account
quizQuestions.explanation: Secure data sharing is the native, no-copy pattern.

quizQuestions.question: A workload has high concurrency but small queries. Best warehouse setup?
quizQuestions.explanation: Multi-cluster warehouses scale out for concurrency.

quizQuestions.question: You need the cheapest staging table with no Fail-safe. Best choice?
quizQuestions.explanation: Transient tables reduce costs and exclude Fail-safe.

quizQuestions.question: You need to track changes for incremental ETL. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: Streams and tasks enable change data capture pipelines.

quizQuestions.question: You need to cap credit usage for a dev warehouse. Best control?
quizQuestions.explanation: Resource monitors control and limit credit usage.

quizQuestions.question: You need to expose only masked columns to support staff. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: Masking policies enforce column-level security.

quizQuestions.question: You need to quickly create a dev copy of production. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: Zero-copy cloning creates fast, storage-efficient copies.

quizQuestions.question: You need to query files in cloud storage without loading. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: External tables query files in external storage without loading.

quizQuestions.question: You need to filter rows based on user context. Best control?
quizQuestions.explanation: Row access policies enforce row-level security.

quizQuestions.question: You need to improve point-lookup performance on a large table. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: Search optimization accelerates selective lookups.

quizQuestions.question: You need to alert when credits exceed 80%. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: Resource monitors can notify at thresholds.

quizQuestions.question: You need a scheduled SQL transformation without external tooling. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: Tasks schedule SQL statements in Snowflake.

quizQuestions.question: You need near real-time processing of stream changes. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: Streams with tasks support near real-time pipelines.

quizQuestions.question: You must restrict login by IP range. Best control?
quizQuestions.explanation: Network policies enforce IP-based access control.

quizQuestions.question: You need reproducible loads and error checking before loading. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: VALIDATION_MODE allows error checking before loading.

quizQuestions.question: You need to avoid duplicate loads in continuous ingestion. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: Snowpipe tracks file metadata to prevent duplicates.

quizQuestions.question: You need to restore a dropped table within retention. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: UNDROP restores objects within Time Travel retention.

quizQuestions.question: You need to share only a subset of columns. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: Secure views limit exposed columns and can be shared.

quizQuestions.question: You need to track lineage for batch jobs. Best design?
quizQuestions.explanation: Query tags combined with history views support lineage tracking.
